meaning: Egnal Enploymcnt Opportunity Commission 均等就业机会委员会
examples: Because the 1972 EEOC ruling was contested in court, the ruling won press attention that popularized maternity-leave policies.
An employee can also file a claim with the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, or EEOC. This process is the same as with other types of discrimination.
The lawsuit against Henry's Turkey Service was filed by the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC).
Jacqueline Berrien was nominated to serve as Chair of the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC).
Yet the EEOC is threatening to launch lawsuits if they do not hire those same felons.
